title: Get ready for the 2026 European Authors Day
content:
The organisers of the European Authors Day invite cultural institutions, businesses, event organisers, educators, local authorities and all interested stakeholders to join the online webinar to learn more about the 2026 edition of the event series.

The hosts of the webinar will discuss:

  • available opportunities in 2026
  • what the 2026 welcome packs will include
  • tips and tricks on how to organise great events

    • Tuesday 16 June 2026 11:30 (CET)

    Join the webinar to learn about the 2026 edition of the European Authors Day.

title: Factsheet: A joint commitment for culture in the European Union
content: Factsheet: A joint commitment for culture in the European Union holbobe Thu, 06/18/2026 - 18:43 Factsheet: A joint commitment for culture in the European Union Available languages (1) Language English
snippet: holbobe Thu, 06/18/2026 - 18:43 Factsheet: A joint commitment for culture in the European Union Available languages (1) Language English
creator: holbobe

title: Europe for Culture – Culture for Europe: a long-term strategic vision for culture in Europe
content:
Europe's three main EU institutions have united behind an unprecedented political commitment to place culture at the centre of the union's strategic agenda.

The European Parliament, the Council of the European Union and the European Commission have signed a Joint Declaration setting out twelve guiding principles for cultural policy across Europe. The declaration positions culture as both a strategic resource for, and a beneficiary of, European unity, democracy and competitiveness.

In an era of rapid change and new challenges for the world, the Joint Declaration places culture at the heart of Europe’s identity by reaffirming its role as a fundamental pillar of European values, resilience and sustainable growth. It recognises culture and cultural heritage as a public good, while acknowledging culture’s intrinsic, societal, civic and economic value, and the EU’s role as a global cultural powerhouse.

Inspired by the Culture Compass for Europe, the Joint Declaration sets out twelve guiding principles for cultural policies in Europe on:

  • Freedom of artistic expression and the right to create
  • Cultural and linguistic diversity
  • Access to culture for all
snippet: Europe's three main EU institutions have united behind an unprecedented political commitment to place culture at the centre of the union's strategic agenda.
creator: holbobe

title: LiveMX supported more than 70 projects on music export, live venues, and digital circulation
content:
Between 2023 and 2026 LiveMX fostered a more diverse and sustainable music sector with a focus on three key areas:

  • music export
  • live music venues
  • digital circulation and engagement

    • Supported by Creative Europe under its Music Moves Europe initiative, LiveMX has redistributed through “cascading grants” €4 million to 72 projects selected through open calls for proposals.

    The selected projects brought together organisations from 26 countries, with activities resonating across more than 40 countries. The calls attracted more than 1 000 applications, showing the high interest and need for this kind of initiatives in the music sector.

    Projects reported tangible benefits, including

  • strengthened cross-border collaboration and network-building
  • greater inclusion and diversity
  • giving more space to marginalised artists/professionals

    • Sustainable practices were also tested, such as slow-touring or testing solutions for energy-efficient venues.

    The project implemented a capacity building programme and an innovative funding scheme to address specific needs within the European music ecosystem.

    Link to LiveMX's website.
snippet:
Between 2023 and 2026 LiveMX fostered a more diverse and sustainable music sector with a focus on three key areas:

music export

live music venues

digital circulation and engagement
creator: csudaga

title: Here is the third edition of Perform Europe
content:
After the publication of the call for proposals, a new consortium has been selected to implement the next phase of Perform Europe, the EU’s scheme that supports touring models in performing arts.

Building on the achievements of the first two phases of Perform Europe, this new phase will identify, and support touring and distribution of at least 25 artistic projects funded through ‘cascading grants’ over the next 24 months. Perform Europe III will receive a grant of €2 million from the Creative Europe programme - Cultural strand.

This funding will enable the projects to engage their audiences through more inclusive and sustainable dissemination methods, benefitting from financial assistance, training and peer-to-peer learning. A digital platform will centralise and promote the supported projects, while also serving as a networking and matchmaking tool.

The objectives of this phase of the scheme are to reduce environmental impact, strengthen fair working conditions, and enhance access for a diversity of audiences.

Perform Europe III will be carried out by a consortium of partners:

  • IETM – the International network for contemporary performing arts
  • European Festivals Association (EFA)
snippet: After the publication of the call for proposals, a new consortium has been selected to implement the next phase of Perform Europe, the EU’s scheme that supports touring models in performing arts.
creator: csudaga

title: Culture Moves Europe: 150 residency projects selected
content:
The combined budget of the call is more than €2.1 million. The grants per project depend on the duration (between 21 and 90 days) and the number of invited artists and cultural professionals (from 1 to 5).

The selected residency projects plan to host 581 artists and cultural professionals from different Creative Europe countries, which are selected in the second phase of the selection procedure. The submissions are reviewed on a rolling basis.

Selected hosts have until 30 September 2026 to select and confirm their residents. In the meantime, projects with early implementation dates can already start from early June 2026. All projects must be concluded by 7 June 2027.

Goethe-Institut is organising online match-making sessions on 5 and 10 June to bring together residency hosts and potential residents.

The 2025-2026 call for residency hosts received 1 326 applications from 38 Creative Europe countries. There will be match-making sessions in early June to bring together residency hosts and potential residents.

The selected residencies are based in 38 countries, including Moldova, that joined the Creative Europe programme in January 2026.

title: World Environment Day: reconnecting with nature through culture
content:
A mural about the ocean, experiencing forest through a creative lens, growing nature in former industrial spaces: across Europe, artists are finding new ways to reconnect people with the environment. 

The European Union supports these initiatives, helping transform ideas into projects with a lasting impact on people, places and nature. On World Environment Day, we put the spotlight on three Creative Europe-funded projects whose work is making a difference on the ground.

Working across coastal regions in Italy, Greece and Portugal, BLUETOUR brought together artists, cultural organisations and local communities to explore how tourism can help protect local heritage and ecosystems rather than put pressure on them.

The project resulted in a series of artworks rooted in local landscapes and identities. In Faro, the mural Cycles by Los Pepes Studio celebrates the rhythms of the Atlantic Ocean while, in Aegean Sea, artist Paolo Lolicata created Poetic Tour of Lesvos, an artistic map encouraging visitors to discover the island through its landscapes and literary heritage.

Together, these works invite residents and visitors alike to engage with places more thoughtfully and sustainably.
snippet: A mural about the ocean, experiencing forest through a creative lens, growing nature in former industrial spaces: across Europe, artists are finding new ways to reconnect people with the environment.
creator: csudaga

title: 2026 European Union Prize for Literature winners announced
content:
On 29 May 2026 the international jury composed of seven literary experts announced the winner of the Grand Prix and the two special mentions of the 2026 European Union Prize for Literature. Emerging authors from 14 countries were shortlisted back in February 2026.

Dora Kaprálová, Czechia: Mariborská hypnóza (Maribor Hypnosis)

Hélène Frédérick, France: Lézardes (Rivers of White)

Vladimir Vujović, Montenegro: Slobodni udarci (Free Kicks)

The winner of the Grand Prix will receive €10 000, as well as financial support for two translations of their work. The two special mention recipients will each receive €5 000 and support for one translation.

The other 11 shortlisted authors will also receive support for one translation. In addition, all shortlisted authors will benefit from promotion at major book fairs across Europe.

The prize is an annual initiative of the Creative Europe programme. It started in 2009 to recognise the best European emerging authors and celebrate the linguistic diversity and excellence of literature in countries participating in the Creative Europe programme.

The jury of the European Union Prize for Literature announced the 2026 winners at the Warsaw Book Fair.
snippet: On 29 May 2026 the international jury composed of seven literary experts announced the winner of the Grand Prix and the two special mentions of the 2026 European Union Prize for Literature. Emerging
creator: csudaga

title: Europe celebrates 2026 heritage award winners in Cyprus
content:
On 28 May, some 500 guests and many more viewers online from the heritage community joined the 2026 European Heritage Awards Ceremony in Nicosia (Cyprus) to celebrate the 30 winners of the European Heritage Awards/ Europa Nostra Awards.

During the ceremony, which formed the highlight of the Cultural Heritage Summit 2026, the laureates of the five Grand Prix and the Public Choice Award selected from this year’s winners were revealed.

Grand Prix and Public Choice Award winners of the European Heritage Awards/ Europa Nostra Awards announced during European Heritage Summit in Nicosia.

The long-term restoration of the Polirone Monastic Complex of San Benedetto Po stabilised earthquake damage, conserved historic fabric, and reactivated the site with new cultural and civic functions.

Find out more about the Polirone Monastic Complex

The Cypriot Fiddler research project documents the life stories of traditional Greek Cypriot and Turkish Cypriot musicians. Through long-term fieldwork, a documentary, a book and an open-access website, it preserves oral storytelling, local dialects and a shared musical heritage.

Find out more about The Cypriot Fiddler

title: European Heritage Awards / Europa Nostra Awards 2026 ceremony
content:
The European Heritage Awards Ceremony 2026 was the highlight of the European Cultural Heritage Summit 2026 taking place from 26 to 30 May in Nicosia, Cyprus.

The event celebrated the 2026 winners, 30 outstanding heritage achievements from 18 European countries.

Five Grand Prix winners and the Public Choice Award winner were announced, each receiving a monetary award of €10 000.

Celebrating the winners of the 2026 European Heritage Awards.

Europa Nostra's dedicated website to the European Heritage Awards.

Celebrating the winners of the 2026 European Heritage Awards.
snippet: The European Heritage Awards Ceremony 2026 was the highlight of the European Cultural Heritage Summit 2026 taking place from 26 to 30 May in Nicosia, Cyprus.
